2004-09-01.title: Hostage Crisis Unfolds in Russia as Guerrillas Seize School
2004-09-01.abstract: Heavily armed insurgents seized a school in southern Russia today and herded scores of schoolchildren and others into its gym.

2004-09-01.title: Beneath Putin's Pedestal, the Ground Keeps Shaking
2004-09-01.abstract: Pres Vladimir Putin's reputation for bringing stability to Russia, source of much of his popular support, has been undermined recently by twin bombings of airliners, significant rebel raids in Chechnya and Ingushetia, political unrest over changes in pension benefits, banking crisis, continuing assault on Yukos Oil and wild fluctuations in markets and investor confidence; photos; suicide bombing near Moscow subway station now adds to turmoil, as long war in Chechnya undermines hopes for longterm stability (M)

2004-09-01.title: Mexico's Leader to Pursue Genocide Case
2004-09-01.abstract: Pres Vicente Fox says that if Mexico's Supreme Court will not hear genocide charges his government has filed against former Pres Luis Echeverria he will propose truth commission to investigate abuses by Institutional Revolutionary Party, which used fraud and corruption to rule for more than seven decades; court threw out special prosecutor's case charging Echeverria, two former aides and three generals in deaths of student protesters in 1971; photos (M)

2004-09-01.title: World's first Internet café hoists a latte to celebrate 10 years
World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Court To Let In Cameras As A Test
2004-09-01.abstract: British Lord Chancellor Lord Falconer announces limited use of television cameras to broadcast court proceedings; originally only judges and lawyers will be able to view footage taken (S)

2004-09-02.title: War and corruption keep Congo tottering
China's 2 Top Leaders Square Off in Contest to Run Policy
2004-09-02.abstract: Hu Jintao and Jiang Zemin, two top Chinese leaders, are engaged in increasingly pointed power struggle that has begun to create cracks in one-party system; Chinese officials and analysts say tension between two has begun to influence debates on issues like slowing overheated economy, fighting corruption and assigning jobs; say most delicate matters are now often viewed within party as battles between rival factions; say Hu is seen as embracing idea that China needs to focus more on populist social problems, while Jiang considers high rate of economic growth as first priority; country's leaders are preparing to gather for national planning session; photo (M)

2004-09-02.title: World Briefing | Asia: Japan: Ex-G.I. To Surrender
2004-09-02.abstract: Former United States soldier Charles Robert Jenkins, who faces court-martial for deserting his unit during Korean War and fleeing to North Korea, will surrender to US military in Japan; is married to Japanese woman, who was kidnapped by North Koreans and now lives in Japan (S)

2004-09-02.title: Panama's New Chief, Sworn In, Inherits a Diplomatic Tempest
2004-09-02.abstract: Martin Torrijos, son of late dictator Omar Torrijos, is sworn in as Panama's new president and quickly confronts diplomatic storm; Cuba broke relations with Panama after departing Pres Mireya Moscoso pardoned four Cuban exiles accused of plotting to assassinate Pres Fidel Castro during visit to Panama in 2000; she says she feared that Torrijos would have them extradited to Cuba, where they would be killed; Torrijos, in his inauguration speech, criticizes Moscoso's decision and says he will try to repair relations with Cuba and with Venezuela, which also expressed outrage about pardons; Secretary of State Colin L Powell attends inauturation but makes few public remarks (M)

2004-09-02.title: Pakistan Found to Aid Iran Nuclear Efforts
2004-09-02.abstract: New assessment of Iran's nuclear program by United Nations' International Atomic Energy Agency says that Pakistan provided Iran with designs of sophisticated centrifuges capable of making bomb-grade nuclear fuel as early as 1995; says Iran recently showed UN inspectors assembled and tested major components for 70 of machines; report provides no new evidence of kind of covert programs that agency has discovered in last year; report suggests that Iranian government is slowly becoming more helpful to inspectors; American officials say that assessment is likely to discourage moves by Bush administration to take Iran to Security Council for penalties unless it dismantles its program (M)

2004-09-03.title: Budget rules to be loosened (officially) : Accepting reality, EU plans changes
Europa : Looking more closely at the message of Sochi
An African Foul-Up, With an Intriguing Cast of Britons
2004-09-03.abstract: Improbable, botched coup in Equatorial Guinea is black eye for Britain because several upper-crust Britons have been implicated, including former Prime Min Margaret Thatcher's son, Sir Mark Thatcher; he was arrested by South African police on charges of illegally helping to finance coup; two of his fellow Etonians are also linked to plot: Simon Mann, now in prison in Zimbabwe, and Jeffrey H Archer, best-selling author and former deputy chairman of Britain's Conservative Party; all three reject any role in plans to overthrow Teodoro Obiang Nguema Mbasoga; his government is among Africa's most repressive, but alleged coup-plotters were said to be more interested in country's oil production; plot fell apart after South African intelligence tipped off Obiang and Zimbabwe police; photos (M)

2004-09-03.title: Ban on Head Scarves Takes Effect in France
2004-09-03.abstract: Law banning Islamic head scarves and other religious symbols from French public schools takes effect peacefully, transforming first day of school into nationwide show of defiance of demand by kidnappers of two French journalists in Iraq that law be rescinded; instead of dividing country, as kidnappers probably had hoped, intellectuals, journalists, religious leaders--even France's Muslims--join forces with government to tell captors of journalists to stay out of France's affairs; photo (M)

2004-09-03.title: Malaysian High Court Voids Convictions of Ex-Deputy Premier
2004-09-03.abstract: High court in Malaysia overturns sodomy conviction of former Deputy Prime Min Anwar Ibrahim and allows him to go free after serving six years in prison; decision, which takes many Malaysians and officials in neighboring countries by surprise, is seen as clearest sign yet of new era since election victory of Prime Min Abdullah Badawi six months ago; Anwar was abruptly fired in 1998 by former leader Mahathir Mohamad over political differences; soon after, he was arrested, tried and convicted on sodomy and corruption charges in what his supporters interpreted as personal revenge by Mahathir against man who was his likely successor; United States welcomes court decision freeing Anwar; question now is whether he can still make comeback in opposition to governing party; his arrest and his sentencing set off demonstrations against Mahathir government; photo (M)

2004-09-04.title: 4 Navy Commandos Are Charged in Abuse
2004-09-04.abstract: Four Navy special forces personnel are charged with abusing Iraqi detainee who later died during questioning at Abu Ghraib prison, and then lying about it; body was packed in ice, then photographed and removed next day to make it appear as if he were only ill; head injury occurred when suspect resisted arrest and Navy Seal struck him with rifle butt; murkiness of incident prevented prosecutors from charging men with manslaughter or homicide; some officers point out that many abuses cited by human rights groups occur at 'point of capture', where dangers are greatest; photo (M)

2004-09-04.title: 250 Die as Siege at a Russian School Ends in Chaos
2004-09-04.abstract: At least 250 people die after two large, unexplained explosions unexpectedly set off pitched battle between Russian forces and heavily armed captors of children at school in Beslan; 531 people are hospitalized, including 283 children, but scores of hostages survive, staggering from school during fight, which lasts several hours; dead include several Russian soldiers and security officials and more than 20 of estimated 30 or more guerrillas; many die when gymnasium roof collapses and burns; officials say Russian forces did not instigate fight but were forced to return fire and storm school after first explosions; some residents join police and army forces in shooting at school and helping fleeing hostages; carnage begins even as negotiators hold intermittent talks with hostage-takers; half of dead hostage-takers are said to be foreigners, seemingly supporting government assertions that Chechen rebels receive aid from abroad; Pres Vladimir Putin visits victims in hospital; orders city sealed and borders blocked in effort to track down any hostage-takers who escaped; photos (L)

2004-09-04.title: Lebanon Agrees to Extend Term of Leader Imposed by Syria
2004-09-04.abstract: Lebanon's Parliament votes, 96 to 29, in extraordinary session to amend Constitution to give three more years to Emile Lahoud, president hand-picked by Syria; heavy-handed effort by Syria to push amendment through generates widespread Lebanese resentment; rush appears to be effort to beat joint American-French resolution in United Nations warning against outside interference in Lebanese elections and calling for removal of all foreign troops; polls show 65 percent of Lebanese opposed to amending Constitution and 74 percent want new president (M)

2004-09-04.title: Russian TV Turns Away From Crisis
2004-09-04.abstract: Russia's three largest television networks turn away from broadcasting climax of Beslan hostage crisis, with two returning to regularly scheduled entertainment programming while fighting continues; Russian networks only resume coverage hours after Western networks beam outcome of siege around the world; Pres Vladimir Putin makes no statement; television coverage seems to lose direction in absence of official statement; critics have long complained that Kremlin's control has limited what Russians see and hear (M)

2004-09-04.title: South African Is Charged With Making Nuclear Components
2004-09-04.abstract: South African police charge engineer Johan Meyer with buying materials and assembling gas centrifuges to purify uranium, apparently for Libya's secret nuclear-bomb program; American diplomats say arrest breaks part of network Pakistani scientist Abdul Qadeer Khan used to sell technology to aspiring nuclear powers; government says charges result from broad inquiry by South Africa, International Atomic Energy Agency and other nations (M)

2004-09-05.title: France Takes Reins of NATO Force in Kosovo at Trying Time
2004-09-05.abstract: French Maj Gen Yves de Kermabon assumes command of NATO force in Kosovo; France also recently assumed command of NATO troops in Afghanistan; American and European diplomats view voluntary commands as way for France to repair damage done to NATO unity by France's opposition to Iraq war; map

2004-09-05.title: Wrecked Russian School Is Opened to Public
2004-09-05.abstract: BESLAN, Russia, Sept. 5 Â Shafts of light passed through the bullet holes in the pupils desks, which had been stacked at windows, makeshift barricades against attack. Grisly scenes awaited behind them.

2004-09-05.title: Warmth Was Wanted; Instead, Putin's Chilly Responses Darken the Political Clouds
2004-09-05.abstract: Many Russians fault Pres Vladimir V Putin's 'chilly response' to recent terrorist attacks; hold that Putin and those around him have failed to express sufficient grief over school attack in Beslan, recent crashes of airliners or attack of subways in Moscow; Putin aims to present himself as steely, decisive leader, but his perceived insensitivity could have profound consequences for his political authority and for country's future; photo (M)

2004-09-05.title: Putin Tells the Russians: 'We Shall Be Stronger'
2004-09-05.abstract: Transcript of Russian Pres Vladimir V Putin's televised remarks at Kremlin on September 4 in wake of recent terrorist attacks on school in Beslan and on passenger airliners; Putin says attacks are aimed at all Russians; links attacks to international terrorism; promises specific steps to prevent future attacks (M)

2004-09-05.title: Putin Says Russia Faces Full 'War' to Divide Nation
2004-09-05.abstract: Pres Vladimir V Putin, in rare address to nation, says school siege in Beslan is attack on all of Russia; calls for society to mobilize to resist effort to splinter nation; speaks as death toll from violent end to hostage crisis rises to 330, half of them children; seeks to answer series of terrorist acts that in 10 wrenching days has killed over 500 people; scores corruption in judiciary and inefficiency of law enforcement and admits difficult transition to capitalism has left few resources to secure borders in dangerous epoch; echoes feeling of helplessness and fear that has shaken nation, without accepting personal responsibility; authorities say terrorists are Islamic militants, mostly Chechens; photo (M)

2004-09-06.title: Car Bomb Kills 7 U.S. Marines and 3 Iraqi Soldiers Near Falluja
2004-09-06.abstract: The U.S. suffered its deadliest attack since May as confusion ended over whether Saddam Hussein's deputy was captured.

2004-09-06.title: Move will precede election; 'long haul' in country expected : NATO set to beef up Kabul force
Plan to Tame Journalists Just Stirs Them Up in Brazil
2004-09-06.abstract: Criticism mounts to Pres Luiz Ignacio Lula da Silva's plan to create national journalists' council to regulate Brazil's boisterous press; news organizations call proposed law serious threat to press freedom; bill would empower council to orient, discipline and monitor journalists and their work (M)

2004-09-06.title: 'No Blood on Our Headscarf'
2004-09-06.abstract: France' Muslims declare their solidarity with the secular state.

2004-09-07.title: Names of the Dead
2004-09-07.abstract: Defense Dept releases name of service member killed in Iraq (S)

2004-09-07.title: Thousands Protest Near Red Square Over Killings at School
2004-09-07.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin vented his anger at the killers and at critics who call for a moderate response.

2004-09-07.title: Before vote, NATO will increase Afghan force
India and Pakistan End 2 Days of Talks; Make Gains on Some Issues but Not on Kashmir
2004-09-07.abstract: Foreign ministers of India and Pakistan conclude two days of talks in New Delhi with modest progress on host of issues and unusually blunt acknowledgment that dispute over Kashmir still looms between them; privately, officials from both sides say they remain hopeful about peace efforts (M)

2004-09-07.title: Muslim-West divide is on Anwar's mind : Return of a Malaysian

2004-09-08.title: THREE YEARS ON: Sept. 11, 2001 : U.S. visa delays sap corporate bottom line
Racial 'Handicaps' and a Great Sprint Forward
2004-09-08.abstract: Liu Xiang is hailed in China as 'the Yellow Bullet' after returning from Athens Olympics with gold medal for 110-meter high hurdles; photo; admiring Chinese say Liu has ovecome 'congenital shortcomings,' proving that 'yellow men' can jump and sprint as well as black and white athletes (M)

2004-09-08.title: British Soldier Charged With Murder of Iraqi
2004-09-08.abstract: British soldier Kevin Lee Williams, who served in tank regiment in Iraq, is charged in London with murdering Iraqi civilian in August 2003, while trying to arrest him (S)

2004-09-08.title: A Groundswell of Sympathy and Donations
2004-09-08.abstract: Planeloads of foreign aid are sent to help Beslan, Russia, but horror of terrorist siege of school evokes outpouring of sympathy across globe; people line up to sign condolence book at embassy in Washington, about 150,000 people hold candlelight march through Rome and world leaders like Prime Min Tony Blair express sorrow (M)

2004-09-09.title: Israeli Military Continues Major Push Into Gaza
2004-09-09.abstract: Israeli forces killed seven Palestinians in Gaza and another in the West Bank.

2004-09-09.title: Massacre Draws Self-Criticism in Muslim Press
2004-09-09.abstract: Brutal school siege in Beslan, Russia, with hundreds of children dead and wounded, touches off unusual round of self-criticism and introspection in Muslim and Arab world; Abdel Rahman al-Rashed, general manager of Al Arabiya satellite television station, writes in pan-Arab newspaper that it is 'shameful and degrading' that Beslan terrorists were Muslims, as were those who carried out other terrorist attacks around world, as well as Osama bin Laden himself; Rashed, like other commentators, singles out Sheik Yusuf al-Qaradawi, senior Egyptian cleric living in Qatar who broadcasts influential program on Al Jazeera and who has issued fatwa, religious ruling, calling for killing of American and foreign 'occupiers' in Iraq, military and civilian (M)

2004-09-09.title: Japan's Record 7th Typhoon; The Death Toll Is at Least 31
2004-09-09.abstract: Japan's typhoon record was broken with a bang this week as battering winds and mountainous waves overturned ocean-going freighters.

2004-09-09.title: In reply, the president vows to honor dead by 'completing mission' : Kerry faults Bush over cost of Iraq war
Russia's Antiterror Tactics: Reward and a First Strike
2004-09-09.abstract: Russian government offers $10 million reward for killing or capture of Chechen rebel leaders Aslan Maskhadov and Shamil Basayev and top general says Moscow reserves right to make pre-emptive strikes against terrorists abroad; several lawmakers propose steps to tighten domestic security; country's chief law enforcement official Prosecutor Gen Vladimir Ustinov offers new official account of attack in Beslan; says band of cutthroat kidnappers argued among themselves and their leader enforced discipline ty executing three of his crew; says not all the attackers realized that mission was to seize school, and one was shot when he objected to kidnapping children; says two women in gang were killed when bombs strapped to their bodies were detonated by remote control by gang leader as form of intimidation; photos (M)

2004-09-10.title: Russian Leader Promises Open Inquiry Into Terror Attack
2004-09-10.abstract: In an unusual retreat, Vladimir V. Putin today promised an open inquiry into the slaughter of hostages at a school.

2004-09-10.title: Italy's hostage response reflects shift in tactics
Letter from Washington : Yet another recovery for the 'Comeback Kid'
Names of the Dead
2004-09-10.abstract: Defense Dept confirms deaths of two service members in Iraq (S)

2004-09-10.title: In Tape, Top Aide To Bin Laden Vows New Strikes at U.S.
2004-09-10.abstract: Osama bin Laden's top deputy Ayman al-Zawahiri appears in new videotape broadcast on Arab television network Al Jazeera, taunting United States on becoming mired in what he calls unsuccessful campaign in Iraq and Afghanistan while vowing that Al Qaeda would attack US again; timing of tape seems intentionally to come just two days before third anniversary of 9/11 attack; unlike other audio or videotapes issued by Qaeda leadership, this one does not include appearance by bin Laden; photo (M)

2004-09-11.title: The Collections / NEW YORK: New run for McCartney
THREE YEARS ON:Sept. 11, 2001 : 3 years after 9/11 attacks, wounds are still not healed
Ties with Germany likely to deteriorate after a unanimous vote in Parliament : Poland demands war reparations
'Holy Warriors' in Samarra Reject Accord With Americans
2004-09-11.abstract: Mujahedeen Shura, or Council of Holy Warriors, claiming to speak for insurgents in contested city of Samarra, rejects agreement allowing American forces and Iraqi government to re-enter city; says its fighters have agreed to merge with Mujahedeen Shura of Falluja; senior cleric Ahmed Abdul Ghafour holds that insurgency seems to be split over question of negotiations; declaration of merger with Falluja insurgents could mark significant development toward united chain of command; map (M)

2004-09-11.title: The Novel's Latest Version Pops Onto China's Cellphones
2004-09-11.abstract: Chinese author Qian Fuzhang (real name He Xingnian) has published latest novel Out of the Fortress as text-message; 4,200-character novel is delivered to mobile phone screens in two daily 70-character installments; author is huge cellphone fan and thought technology could provide new ways of reading; has received advance of more than $20,000 for book, which tells story of an extramarital affair; some critics call it cheapening of literature, but another critic marvels at 'inventive combination' of Chinese characters; photos (M)

2004-09-12.title: Greek Defense Minister Quits for Deadly Copter Crash
2004-09-12.abstract: The resignation came a day after Africa's senior Greek Orthodox prelate and 16 others died in a helicopter crash in the northern Aegean.

2004-09-12.title: Greek Orthodox Patriarch Dies With 12 Others in Copter Crash
2004-09-12.abstract: Greek Orthodox Patriarch Petros VII of Alexandria dies along with dozen church officials in helicopter crash just minutes from landing at Mt Athos in northern Greece; bodies of patriarch and six others are recovered; cause of crash is unknown; photo (M)

2004-09-12.title: Military Specialist Pleads Guilty to Abuse and Is Jailed
2004-09-12.abstract: Army Specialist Armin J Cruz pleads guilty to abusing prisoners at Abu Ghraib prison in Baghdad; is demoted to private and sentenced to eight months in jail; is first intelligence analyst to be tried in Abu Ghraib case; offers no excuse, but says he was affected by loss of comrades in mortar attack earlier that month; photo (M)

2004-09-12.title: Rivers Run Black, and Chinese Die of Cancer
2004-09-12.abstract: Article in series The Great Divide, examining widening gap between China's urban rich and rural poor, focuses on Huai River basin, vast system of waterways that has become grossly polluted waste outlet for thousands of factories in central China; 150 million people live in Huai basin, many of them peasants now threatened by water too toxic to touch, much less to drink, and by rising rates of cancer; one of biggest polluters is Lianhua Gourmet Powder Co, China's largest producer of flavor-enhancer monosodium glutamate, whose political influence is so vast that environmental regulators who have tried to challenge company have done so in vain; 10th anniversary of government's promise to clean up Huai has become major embarrassment for Communist Party; over $8 billion has been spent to improve basin, but regulators have concluded that some areas are more polluted than ever; map; photos (M)

2004-09-12.title: Atomic Activity in North Korea Raises Concerns
2004-09-12.abstract: President Bush and his top advisers have received intelligence reports in recent days describing a confusing series of actions by North Korea that some experts believe could indicate the country is preparing to conduct its first test explosion of a nuclear weapon, according to senior officials with access to the intelligence.    While the indications were viewed as serious enough to warrant a warning to the White House, American intelligence agencies appear divided about the significance of the new North Korean actions, much as they were about the evidence concerning Iraq's alleged weapons stockpiles.

2004-09-13.title: U.N. Envoy Seeks to Enlist the Clergy to Ease Conflicts
2004-09-13.abstract: American ambassador to United Nations John C Danforth says he is trying to create new forum for leaders of world's faiths to involve themselves in resolving conflicts in world of terror; holds that many conflicts in world today are between nations and people who feel they are commanded by God to shoot children and blow up buses; says he is deeply troubled by silence from religious leaders in face of acts like recent school siege in Russia in which hostage takers attacked students trying to flee; Danforth, an Episcopal minister and former US senator, says there is nowhere to bring forth religious grievances and have them publicly discussed and, possibly, reconciled; photo (M)

2004-09-13.title: Military Shake-Up After Crash That Killed an Orthodox Prelate
2004-09-13.abstract: Greek Defense Min Spilios Spiliotopoulos dismisses country's air force chief and then tenders his own resignation day after Petros VII, Africa's senior Greek Orthodox prelate and 16 others die in helicopter crash in northern Aegean Sea; rescue operations did not begin for more than two hours after contact was lost; opposition socalist party Pasok says faster response might have lowered death toll; Prime Min Costas Caramanlis lays blame for tardy rescue effort squarely on military; military sources say impact was so severe that there was probably little left of helicopter; Petros's body has been recovered; photo (M)

2004-09-13.title: Pathbreaking Magazine Again in Peril in Indonesia
2004-09-13.abstract: Verdict in suit filed by Indonesian entrepreneur Tomy Winata against Bambang Harymurti, chief editor of Tempo magazine, is due on Sept 16; Tempo was shut down in 1994 because of its reporting on government corruption and flourishes today in young democracy, although nation's antiquated press laws makes its continuation doubtful; libel laws include criminal penalties and date from colonial period; magazine's history discussed (M)

2004-09-13.title: Giscard says 'a rule we can't change' hurts Ankara's chances : Will Turkey join the EU club?
After School Siege, Russia Also Mourns Secret Heroes
2004-09-13.abstract: At least 11 members of Russia's most elite special forces teams died trying to rescue hundreds of hostages in deadly climax to attack at school in Beslan; precise circumstances of their deaths remain unexplained, fueling bitter questions about whether Russian government tactics may have cost lives; questions also put spotlight on some of country's most covert military units; even in death government withholds their names as they are buried in little-known section of huge cemetery outside of Moscow; photos (M)

2004-09-14.title: World Briefing | Asia: Nepal: U.S. To Give $1 Million Against Rebels
2004-09-14.abstract: United States Embassy in Katmandu, Nepal, three days after Maoist bombing that targeted American government building, makes request for additional $1 million from State Department to assist Nepalese government with security (S)

2004-09-14.title: In a Fight Over King Crab, Russians Show Their Claws
2004-09-14.abstract: Kamchatka, rugged, remote peninsula famous for its red king crab, is deprived of its mainstay by decision of federal government in faraway Moscow, which allotted this year's quotas for catching king crab in coastal waters, rather than auctioning quotas off as in past; as result, Kamchatka's biggest fishery companies received none; decision can be attributed to convoluted government bureaucracy, vagaries of Russia's feral form of capitalism, and corruption; adding to problem is that catch is shrinking; photo; map (M)

2004-09-14.title: Washington Offers Guarded Critique of Putin's Plans
2004-09-14.abstract: The secretary of state said today that terror should not become an excuse to move away from democratic reforms.

2004-09-14.title: China's new fashion whispers but does not shout 'Asia'
World Briefing | Europe: Kosovo: 2,000 More NATO Troops
2004-09-14.abstract: Kosovo NATO peacekeeping force will gain additional 2,000 troops ahead of next month's parliamentary elections; troops will be supplied by Italy, France and Germany (S)

2004-09-15.title: Big nations 'examine' Russian's proposals; others are critical : EU divided in reaction to Putin's power play
World Briefing | Europe: The Hague: Milosevic's Lawyers Ask For Trial Suspension
2004-09-15.abstract: Newly court-appointed British defense lawyers representing Slobodan Milosevic ask court to suspend trial while Milosevic appeals appointment of council by United Nations judges (S)

2004-09-15.title: Israelis Raid Hideouts in West Bank, Killing 9 Palestinians
2004-09-15.abstract: Israeli troops killed nine Palestinians today in two separate raids on militant hideouts in the northern West Bank.

2004-09-15.title: Americans seem not to focus on the war
World Briefing | Europe: Northern Ireland: Sinn Fein Finds Bugging Device
2004-09-15.abstract: Sinn Fein, political party linked with Irish Republican Army, finds listening device at Belfast headquarters one week after party president Gerry Adams' assistant discovered bug at his home; Adams says he will present evidence to British Prime Minister Tony Blair and Irish Prime Minister Bertie Ahern at upcoming meeting to be held at Leeds Castle, England (S)

2004-09-16.title: Sharon Doubts Gaza Exit Aids the Road Map
2004-09-16.abstract: Prime Min Ariel Sharon acknowledges that Israel is not following moribund Middle East peace plan and says Israeli pullout from Gaza Strip is unlikely to revive it; makes comment on one of deadliest days of past year in West Bank, as Israeli troops kill nine Palestinian militants in two raids on hide-outs; Palestinians claim young girl was fatally shot in one raid (M)

2004-09-16.title: Strife Drops, and Belfast Builds Again With Hope
2004-09-16.abstract: Winds of change are blowing in Northern Ireland, 10 years after outlawed Irish Republican Army declared cease-fire in its war to end British rule; there are still daily signs of sectarian hatred, fear and crime, but some parts of province are in increasingly visible recovery; Belfast, once battleground of rubble, is now vibrant city of commerce and night life, and soaring architecture of glass and steel; photos; map (M)

2004-09-16.title: Mercenaries in Afghan Case Get 8 to 10 Years in Prison
2004-09-16.abstract: Three Americans are sentenced in Kabul, Afghanistan, to 8 to 10 years in prison for running private jail there and torturing prisoners; panel of three Afghan judges rejects their claim that they were working for Pentagon counterterrorist group; three are Jonathan K Idema, Brent Bennett and Edward Caraballo; they say they were abandoned by their American masters when they became political liability; lawyers for Americans try unsuccessfully to show videotape as evidence that Idema had relationship with counterterrorism officials in American military; taped conversations handed out to journalists by lawyers could have provided evidence that men were working for some special unit with knowledge and cooperation of people in Pentagon, but there is no immediate way to verify authenticity of tapes; photo (M)

2004-09-16.title: The Thriller From America That Has the French in Thrall
2004-09-16.abstract: French translation of American megaselling book The Da Vinci Code, which is set mostly in Paris, has sold so well that at least dozen tour operators, art historians and self-appointed experts are cashing in, offering insider views of sites, symbols, legends and lore featured on its pages; photo of American tourists at site prominently mentioned in book (M)

2004-09-16.title: Annan Urges Security Council to Act Quickly on Sudan
2004-09-16.abstract: UNITED NATIONS, Sept. 16 Â Intervening personally in an ongoing Security Council debate, Secretary General Kofi Annan urged the panel today to act quickly on a resolution to curb the violence in Sudan's Darfur region and said he was eager to set up a commission called for in the measure to investigate whether genocide had occurred.

2004-09-17.title: IRAQ STUDY FINDS DESIRE FOR ARMS, BUT NOT CAPACITY
2004-09-17.abstract: New report by American weapons inspector Charles A Duelfer is said to conclude that Saddam Hussein's government had clear intent to produce nuclear, chemical and biological weapons if United Nations sanctions were lifted, but that there is no evidence that Iraq had begun any such program by time of American invasion of Iraq; government officials say most specific evidence of illicit weapons program has been uncovered in clandestine labs operated by Iraqi Intelligence Service, which could produce small quantities of lethal chemical and biological agents, probably for use in assassinations, not to inflict mass casualties; say Duelfer's report essentially reaffirms findings of interim review 11 months ago, but adds considerable detail on question of Iraq's intentions if UN penalties were weakened or lifted; Duelfer is top American weapons inspector in Iraq; final version of his report is expected to be made public in next several weeks; Republicans and Democrats are likely to seize on separate aspects of report in effort to score political points; Pres Bush will cite Duelfer's conclusion that Hussein sought to acquire illicit weapons; Sen Kerry will highlight report's conclusion that Iraq did not possess stockpiles of illicit weapons, which was Bush's major rationale for war; photo (M)

2004-09-17.title: Delegation is led by British ambassador : Foreign diplomats view North Korean blast site
Christian Democrats propose 'third way' to benefit all parties : Germans oppose Turkey's EU entry
Pyongyang stalls on next round of talks on suspected arsenal : Envoys view North Korean blast site
Ryder Cup is golf's trans-Atlantic slugfest : Teed up and ready to roar

2004-09-18.title: Rightists hope strife pulls votes in Saxony
N.B.A. Cancels Game in Russia, Citing Terrorism Risk
2004-09-18.abstract: National Basketball Assn cancels Oct 8 exhibition game in Russia featuring Utah Jazz against Moscow club CSKA, citing recent terrorist attacks; will work to reschedule game; CSKA had given NBA security assurances and is disappointed that game has been canceled (M)

2004-09-18.title: A Mild Shanghai Lawyer and His Accidental Crusade
2004-09-18.abstract: Profile of Shanghai attorney Guo Guoting, who has joined long and growing roll of accidental activists in China who are driven to do something about intolerable injustices they encounter in their own lives; Guo stepped forward to defend classmate from law school who was jailed for filing lawsuits on behalf of Shanghai residents arbitrarily dispossessed by local authorities in interests of urban development; lost case and was driven from law practice after government officials scared his clients away; photo (M)

2004-09-19.title: Militant Group in Iraq Claims It Beheaded 3 Men
2004-09-19.abstract: The militants posted a gruesome video of the beheading of three men said to be members of a Kurdish political party in Iraq.

2004-09-19.title: G.O.P. Senators Voice Rising Concerns on Iraq
2004-09-19.abstract: The comments came as the interim Iraqi prime minister, Ayad Allawi, was telling a television interviewer that "we are winning."

2004-09-19.title: For Hussein, a Spartan Life at His Former Palace
2004-09-19.abstract: Saddam Hussein, pulled disheveled and disoriented from underground bunker by American troops last Dec, is living in air-conditioned 10-by-13 foot cell on grounds of one of his former palaces outside Baghdad; has been interrogated by investigators preparing for his trial on charges of genocide and crimes against humanity; denies any wrongdoing and shows no remorse for killing of hundreds of thousands of people during his dictatorship; insists he remains president; conditions of his captivity noted; photos (M)

2004-09-19.title: Iran Rejects U.N. Call to Freeze Nuclear Enrichment
2004-09-19.abstract: Iran also warned that it would drop out of the nonproliferation treaty if its case is sent to the Security Council.

2004-09-19.title: Blair Tries to Counter Iraq Skeptics in Europe
2004-09-19.abstract: Prime Minister Tony Blair argued that Iraq is the "crucible" in which global terrorism will either succeed or founder.

2004-09-20.title: Effort to Train New Iraqi Army Is Facing Delays
2004-09-20.abstract: Military command in charge of one of highest priority missions in Iraq, training and equipping Iraqi security forces, has fewer then half of its permanent headquarters personnel in place, three months into its new mission; military officials say effect of headquarters' shortages on actual training of Iraqi forces is hard to measure, but gaps do mean fewer people to lobby Washington for resources, coordinate with Iraqi officials and get money and equipment into hands of trainers around country; delay in filling headquarters jobs said to stem from Pentagon's methodical approach to establishing new organization with extremely complex mission of preparing more than 250,000 members of Iraqi police, border patrol, national guard and army units for duty; group's commander Lt Gen David H Petraeus is taking steps to ensure that training and equipping Iraqi forces continues smoothly; has borrowed top lawyers, training experts and other specialists from Pentagon, West Point, American commands worldwide and even from British forces in Iraq to tide him over until permanent staff arrives (M)

2004-09-20.title: New York gains ground in bid for 2012 Games
Israel Launches Airstrike in Gaza City
2004-09-20.abstract: An Israeli airstrike in Gaza City, the second in as many days, killed two Hamas militants and wounded at least six bystanders, Palestinians said.

2004-09-20.title: In Ethnic Tinderbox, Fear of Revenge for School Killings
2004-09-20.abstract: People of Kartsa, little Russian town not far from Beslan, fear reprisal for school siege and killings two weeks ago; Kartsa people are ethnic Ingush, while victims of killings were mostly Ossetians; feuds and hatreds between two groups go back too far to calculate; although none of attackers have been identified as Ingush, people of Beslan are certain they were responsible and rumors are flying in Kartsa about coming attack from Ossetians; Kartsa still bears scars from last time two peoples fought, in brief, brutal war 12 years ago; photos; map (M)

2004-09-21.title: Seeking antitrust job, she vows never to return to board seats : EU's Kroes confronts conflicts of interest
In Eastern Germany, it all comes down to jobs
The Collections / LONDON : Urban versus country:The hunt is on
Beheading of Second American Is Reported by Islamist Web Site
2004-09-21.abstract: An Islamist group said it had beheaded the second of two Americans it seized with a Briton last week, according to a Web site that has proven reliable.

2004-09-21.title: In Address at U.N., Bush Defends Decision to Invade Iraq
2004-09-21.abstract: President Bush told skeptical foreign leaders and envoys today that Iraq was on its way to stability and democracy.

2004-09-22.title: U.S. to sell 'bunker busters' to Israel
Iran Moves Toward Enriching Uranium
2004-09-22.abstract: Iran defies International Atomic Energy Agency, announcing it has begun converting tons of uranium into gas, crucial step toward making fuel for nuclear reactor or bomb; diagram; announcement defies UN agency's call for halt to all uranium-enrichment activities and adds weight to US calls for UN Security Council action (M)

2004-09-22.title: Islamist Web Site Reports Beheading of Second American
2004-09-22.abstract: Islamist terrorist group, led by Abu Musab al-Zarqawi, posts Internet message announcing beheading of second American hostage, presumably Jack Hensley; photos of Hensley and of blast damage in Baghdad; body of Eugene Armstrong is found near his home in Baghdad; US military opens intense operation in Sadr City slum, attacking Moktada al-Sadr's fighters; two marines reported killed in Anbar; US soldiers raid Sadr office in Najaf, arresting some dozen aides (M)

2004-09-22.title: Schröder to ask for Afghan extension
World Briefing | Middle East: Israel: Strike Halts Air And Sea Traffic
2004-09-22.abstract: General strike in Israel is called in support of municipal workers who have not been paid in months; is led by main labor federation Histadrut; 400,000 public workers take part in job action (S)

2004-09-23.title: Seoul puts 2,800 troops into northern Iraq base
Asia Letter : How will the general use his huge mandate?
World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Protest Against Regional Leader
2004-09-23.abstract: Eighty-nine Russians are arrested and several injured during protest by thousands in Elista, Kalmykia; demonstrators call for resignation of Kalmykia President Kirsan Ilyumzhinov (S)

2004-09-24.title: Proposal by Italy and Germany gains despite opposition : EU to study transit sites in Libya for immigrants
Powell Holds Brief Meeting With Minister From Libya
2004-09-24.abstract: Colin L Powell becomes first American secretary of state to meet with Libyan envoy since at least 1970's; he meets with Foreign Min Abdurahman Shalgam while both men are in New York for United Nations General Assembly; Pres Bush just revoked trade embargo on Libya and took other steps in return for Libya's dismantling of its unconventional weapons programs; Powell reportedly outlined to Shalgam what Libya must do to further improve relations with Washington (M)

2004-09-24.title: Top Oil Official Slain as Violence Continues to Churn in Iraq
2004-09-24.abstract: Sana Toma Suleiman, senior Iraqi oil official, is shot and killed in Mosul after surviving two earlier assassination attempts; insurgents have been tenacious in attacking oil infrastructure of Iraq and people crucial to state-run industry; killing comes as violence continues in hot spots around Iraq; confusion continues to swirl around fate of two Italian aid workers kidnapped from their homes in Baghdad on Sept 7; fate of another hostage, Kenneth Bigley of Britain, remains unknown; photos (M)

2004-09-24.title: World Briefing | Europe: Greece: Review Of Euro Data
2004-09-24.abstract: European Commission spokesman holds that despite recent announcement by Greek officials that financial figures may have been tweaked around time Greece met criteria to use euro Greece will continue to be eligible for currency (S)

2004-09-24.title: Dominican Retires Rich
2004-09-24.abstract: Former Pres Hipolito Mejia of Dominican Republic, who lost his bid for re-election, left office a millionaire even as most of his countrymen saw deepening poverty (S)

2004-09-25.title: Brazil Pressing for Favorable Treatment on Nuclear Fuel
2004-09-25.abstract: Brazil hopes to begin operating uranium enrichment plant this year, arguing that it deserves 'dignified and differentiated' treatment, recognizing that it is not a rogue state and its nuclear program is peaceful; International Atomic Energy Agency is seeking agreement on unimpeded access to enrichment factory in Recende, but Brazil is balking at granting access to technological aspects; Brazilian plan poses challenge for Western nations seeking to rein in Iranian nuclear program; Brazil, which has fifth-largest known reserves of uranium, ultimately hopes export enriched uranium, but some independent specialists consider those ambitions unrealistic and note glut of uranium on market (M)

2004-09-25.title: To Help Iraq Vote, Powell Seeks Meeting of Nations
2004-09-25.abstract: Bush administration is working with Iraqi interim Prime Min Ayad Allawi to press Western democracies and several Middle Eastern countries to hold conference in October to bolster Iraqi election process; officials insist timing of conference, if it occurs, has nothing to do with American election; most countries in region and Europe favor idea of conference but are wary of bolstering political fortunes of Allawi or Bush (M)

2004-09-25.title: Envoy Suggests Sharing Power to Aid Darfur
2004-09-25.abstract: United Nations High Comr for Refugees Ruud Lubbers suggests limited autonomy could quell rebellion in Sudan's Darfur region; praises Sudan's commitment to ending aerial bombing and giving Janjaweed militias signal to stop attacking villages; Sudanese official expresses willingness to discuss autonomy, but American official points out that Sudan has already achieved much of its goal of clearing Darfur of its African communities; photo (M)

2004-09-25.title: Wildlife's revenge : Even in urban Singapore, it can sometimes be a jungle
World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Airport Gains Reprieve
2004-09-25.abstract: Berlin city court suspends order to close Tempelhof, historic downtown airport, pending hearing (S)

2004-09-26.title: 6-Nation North Korean Nuclear Talks in Doubt
2004-09-26.abstract: Political analysts voice doubt that multinational talks on North Korea's weapons program will resume as planned this week; talks involve North and South Korea, Japan, China, Russia and United States; North Korea's Foreign Ministry has issued statement linking its future participation in nuclear talks to meeting in early Nov of International Atomic Energy Agency after agency team completes second inspection of South Korean nuclear facilities; talks with Nortj Jorea involve

2004-09-26.title: Hamas Official Killed in Syria
2004-09-26.abstract: An official of the militant organization Hamas was killed when his sport utility vehicle exploded in the Syrian capital.

2004-09-26.title: With a Raceway, China Motors Toward the Modern Age
2004-09-26.abstract: Formula One auto racing will arrive in Shanghai, China, after concerted government effort that began in 2001; construction of 3.3-mile track on former swampland 18 miles west of Shanghai cost $300 million; it includes seating for 200,000 spectators under sleek, wing-shaped roof; design is considered one of most technically advanced and challenging on Formula One circuit; world's top drivers will compete; Michael Schumacher, reigning Formula One champion, claims new track is best he has ever seen; photo; map (M)

2004-09-26.title: Mexico Shuts Tough-Love Center
2004-09-26.abstract: Mexican authorities shut down Casa by the Sea in Ensenada, behavior-modification program for American adolescents; say youths were in violation of Mexican immigration statutes, that program was improperly dispensing pharmaceuticals and that four children showed signs of abuse; program is run by World Wide Assn of Specialty Programs and Schools, which denies Mexican charges (S)

2004-09-26.title: Killings Surge in Iraq, and Doctors See a Procession of Misery
2004-09-26.abstract: Morgue in Baghdad receives 20 to 25 bodies on average day, human cost of post-war wave of crime and insurgency engulfing city; murder rate rose this summer, after more than year of promises from American authorities about increasing effectiveness of Iraqi police and security forces; photos; graph (M)

2004-09-27.title: Names of the Dead
2004-09-27.abstract: Defense Dept confirms death of four service members in Iraq (S)

2004-09-27.title: Turkey Revises Its Penal Code in Bid to Join the European Union
2004-09-27.abstract: Turkish Parliament approves sweeping revision of country's penal code to bring it closer to European Union norms; package makes no mention of criminalizing adultery, move Prime Min Recep Tayyip Erdogan had initially supported (M)

2004-09-27.title: U.S. Military Arrests an Iraqi Commander
2004-09-27.abstract: American military announces arrest of senior commander of nascent Iraqi National Guard, saying Brig Gen Talib Abid Ghayib al-Lahibi is suspected of having association with known insurgents; arrest raises concerns about loyalty and reliability of new security forces just months before general elections; photo (M)

2004-09-27.title: Thailand Investigates Possible Bird Flu
2004-09-27.abstract: HONG KONG, Sept. 27 - Officials in Thailand announced today that a 32-year-old woman had been hospitalized with avian influenza and that two of her family members had already died of a flu-like illness, raising the possibility that these might be among the first case of human-to-human transmission.

2004-09-27.title: Army May Reduce Length of Tours in Combat Zones
2004-09-27.abstract: Senior Army personnel officers say recruitment and troop retention will fall unless current 12-month combat tours in Iraq and Afghanistan are shortened to between six and nine months, but Army officials involved in combat operations and war planning cite need to maintain current troop strength in both countries; Army planners say they must prepare for possibility that it will be necessary to keep troops at current levels in Iraq--138,000--through 2007, even though no political decision has been made in that regard; prospect of lengthy combat tours already appears to be affecting recruitment; National Guard is expected to miss its signup goal this year for first time in decade; Army has largely deployed forces in overseas combat situations in six-month tours of duty since Vietnam War; 12-month deployment was introduced last year after end of major combat operations in Iraq, when vigorous insurgency persuaded military that it would need to maintain large numbers of troops in country; Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld is said to be major force driving consideration of shorter combat tours (M)

2004-09-28.title: 2 Suspects in Murder of Journalist Are Arrested
2004-09-28.abstract: Officials provided few details about the men or their motive, and Russia's chief prosecutor later suggested the arrests were disclosed prematurely.

2004-09-28.title: Collapse at airport injures dozen others : 5 are killed as wall in Dubai falls
Italy Rejoices at Release of Aid Workers in Iraq
2004-09-28.abstract: Italy rejoiced today at the safe release of the young Italian aid workers known in Rome as "the two Simonas."

2004-09-28.title: Thais Suspect Human Spread of Bird Flu
2004-09-28.abstract: Two members of Thai family fall ill with avian influenza, and third probably died of it, prompting Thai health ministry to announce that human-to-human transmission of deadly illness has probably occurred for first time this year; biggest worry of public health officials has been that virus would evolve into form readily transmissible among people that would allow it to spread around globe within weeks; Thai health officials say there is no evidence that virus mutated; photos (M)

2004-09-28.title: Jordan's King Says 'Chaos' in Iraq Rules Out January Vote
2004-09-28.abstract: King Abdullah said he was concerned that this would leave the Sunni Arab minority, which controls a number of cities in Iraq, out of the voting.

2004-09-29.title: Blair Offers an Apology, of Sorts, Over Iraq
2004-09-29.abstract: Prime Min Tony Blair offers his governing Labor Party qualified apology that evidence about Iraq having actual weapons of mass destruction turned out to be wrong; tells annual party conference in Brighton that he is fallible, but that terrorism has profoundly changed international affairs; photos; says nobody could have anticipated spending time on how terrorists trained in remote Hindu Kush ended up threatening British way of life; calls for addressing causes, promising to make revival of Israel-Palestinian talks personal priority after US elections; Blair, leading party toward May election, lays out domestic agenda with ten specific pledges (M)

2004-09-29.title: Rockets Kill 2 Israelis and Prompt Deadly Gaza Clashes
2004-09-29.abstract: A Palestinian rocket killed two Israeli children today, and Israeli forces killed four Palestinians during a day of bloodshed along the northern edges of the Gaza Strip.

2004-09-29.title: Ecological protest turns 'waste' into a fashion statement
Nomad designers:always on a fast plane, to the office

2004-09-30.title: Spreading Scandinavian Genes, Without Viking Boats
2004-09-30.abstract: Sperm banking has become powerhouse industry in Denmark because it has high success rate in producing offspring, because its secular and sexually liberal culture holds uncomplicated view of sperm donation and its laws continue to protect donor's anonymity; Cryos International of Arhus, Denmark, aggressively markets its sperm banks around world; photos; map (M)

2004-09-30.title: The Collections / Milan : Prada flies ahead of the flock
The Collections / Milan : India is all the Raj as designers look for exotic destinations
Foreign Minister Says Iran Is Using Atoms for Peace
2004-09-30.abstract: Foreign Min Kamal Kharrazi of Iran says his country would never give up its right to develop nuclear technology for peaeceful use; denies any intent to produce nuclear weapons; says Iran is ready to negotiate with European countries to find way to calm fears that it is developing nuclear weapons; Iran recently defied International Atomic Energy Agency by saying it is resuming enrichment of uranium, but to produce electric power, not bombs as United States has charged; US is pushing to bring matter before United Nations Security Council (M)

2004-09-30.title: Tony Blair to Undergo Heart Procedure
2004-09-30.abstract: Prime Minister Tony Blair said that he would enter the hospital on Friday for a "routine" medical procedure to correct an irregular heartbeat.
